name: ARS — Auto Replenishment
description: Distributor replenishment agent for CPG-OS: ideal-order recommendation, modification review, fund coverage, and ERP push.
longDescription: ARS 2.0 is the Auto-Replenishment agent under FieldAssist CPG-OS. It exposes ~12 typed actions through CLI, API, UI, and MCP surfaces. Domain modules are wired through 7 pluggable strategy interfaces (Forecast, Quantity, Allocation, Approval, Scheduling, Lifecycle, Truncation). Built on .NET 9 (engine/API/CLI/MCP), Python 3.12 (ML forecasting), and Nuxt 4 (console UI).

scoreNotes.latency: Tier-S load test passed at 30 rps with P95 37ms on recommend_replenishment.
scoreNotes.safety: Eval gate includes fail-closed contract lock verification before ERP push.
scoreNotes.toolUse: 12 typed actions across CLI, API, UI, and MCP surfaces.

evalSuites.name: F-EVALGATE — Pre-Registered Evaluation Gate
evalSuites.description: Forecasting gate correctness: WAPE, MASE, bootstrap CI, tiering, determinism

cases.name: Lock fail closed
cases.description: Contract lock verification fails closed on mismatch

evalSuites.name: Tier-S Load Test
evalSuites.description: 30 rps sustained for 5 min against real Postgres — P95 < 2000ms SLO

gtm.tagline: Automate distributor replenishment with auditable, strategy-pluggable intelligence
gtm.valueProposition: Generate ideal orders, validate distributor modifications, check fund coverage, and push to ERP — all through a normalized CLI · API · UI · MCP contract under CPG-OS.

name: Outlet Dedupe
description: Detects duplicate CPG outlet records using storefront-photo similarity, geo, phone, GSTIN, and reviewable evidence.
longDescription: Headless outlet-deduplication agent for CPG field-sales teams. Tier M foundation with API + UI + CLI + MCP over one Python application/core layer. Finds duplicate or low-quality outlet records, explains the evidence, and prepares safe recommendations to retire duplicates or recapture poor photos. Works standalone or as a CPG-OS action agent.

scoreNotes.latency: Photo similarity and VLM corroboration add latency; load test not yet verified.
scoreNotes.safety: Human-in-the-loop retirement flow, VLM evidence checks, and tenant auth scoping.
scoreNotes.toolUse: API, MCP, CLI, and UI surfaces over a shared Python core.

evalSuites.description: Manifest, API, MCP, and A2A contract conformance

evalSuites.description: Photo evidence, VLM verification, auth scoping, FA native handover

gtm.tagline: Clean outlet master data with photo-grounded duplicate detection
gtm.valueProposition: Find duplicate or low-quality outlet records, explain the evidence, and prepare safe retire-or-recapture recommendations — via UI, API, CLI, MCP, or CPG-OS orchestration.
useCases: Duplicate outlet detection across photo, geo, and metadata signals
CPG-OS action agent for outlet-quality signals

name: Micromarket
longDescription: React, Vite, and Node.js application for micromarket and geospatial analysis. The app serves a built frontend and Express API from one container. - Frontend: React, TypeScript, Vite - Backend: Node.js, Express, TypeScript via `tsx` - Runtime port: `3001`

name: Perfect Store
description: Depth-specialist agent for CPG-OS that validates Perfect Store KPI anomalies with deterministic evidence and authors reversible, HITL-gated Plugs for FA rule/focus area deployment. Diagnoses 21 diagnosable KPIs (Sales, Coverage, Discipline, MT, Campaign, Supply Chain) against con
longDescription: Depth-specialist agent for **CPG-OS** that validates Perfect Store KPI anomalies with deterministic evidence and authors reversible, HITL-gated Plugs for FieldAssist rule/focus area deployment. The CPG-OS supervisor senses anomalies and hands hypotheses to this agent. This agent validates them against 21 diagnosable KPIs using deterministic math, returns a verdict (`confirm | refute | inconclusive`) with typed outputs (`Observation | Diagnosis | Opportunity | Plug`), and — on approval — executes the Plug against FA tables. **One agent, one job:** validate Perfect Store KPI hypotheses and autho

gtm.valueProposition: Depth-specialist agent for CPG-OS that validates Perfect Store KPI anomalies with deterministic evidence and authors reversible, HITL-gated Plugs for FA rule/focus area deployment. Diagnoses 21 diagnosable KPIs (Sales, Coverage, Discipline, MT, Campaign, Supply Chain) against contextual thresholds. .NET 9 + Parquet on Azure Blob + Nuxt 4. Tier S
useCases: Supervisor hands a hypothesis; agent validates it with deterministic KPI computation and returns a verdict with typed evidence.
On a confirmed diagnosis, agent authors a reversible, HITL-gated Perfect Store rule/focus-area configuration as a Plug.
Executes an approved Plug against FA Perfect Store tables and emits a RunResult with changes[].
Measures the KPI impact of a deployed intervention (pre/post) when the supervisor delegates outcome measurement.

name: Agent Store
description: Catalog, score, and launch agents across your product portfolio — technical evals, GTM readiness, and builder leaderboards in one hub.
longDescription: Agent Store is the central hub for product teams to discover, evaluate, and launch agents. It catalogs agents with search and filters, tracks weighted technical scores (reliability, latency, tool use, safety, maintainability, documentation), monitors eval pass rates per suite, and surfaces GTM readiness checklists. Teams can import agents from GitHub repos, compare portfolios side-by-side, and manage metadata through a dashboard. Built on Next.js 16 with Vercel Blob persistence for production CRUD.

scoreNotes.toolUse: GitHub repo import, REST API for agent CRUD, and Vercel Blob persistence.
scoreNotes.documentation: Scoring methodology page, agent definition guide, and deploy README.
scoreNotes.safety: No auth on dashboard yet — team-only access pending.

gtm.tagline: Discover, evaluate, and launch every agent in your portfolio
gtm.valueProposition: One place to catalog agents, track technical scores and eval pass rates, monitor GTM readiness, and compare builders — with GitHub import and a team dashboard.
useCases: Catalog all team agents with status and category filters
Import agent metadata from GitHub repositories
Compare agents across technical, eval, and GTM dimensions
Track builder scoreboard by agent owner
Monitor GTM launch checklists per agent

readinessChecklist.item: Agent catalog with search and filters
